steps.title: Identify the ambivalence marker
steps.content: Look for the ONE strongest signal: a contrast structure that simultaneously holds both displeasure and appreciation, showing the speaker's conflicting attitudes.

steps.title: Apply the contrast to the choices
steps.content:
Choice D pairs displeasure (perfect contempt for it) with the discovery of merit (there is...a place for the genuine), using however to bridge the contradiction.

The other three present either criticism without merit-acknowledgment (B, C) or appreciation without displeasure (A).

That matches choice D — D is correct.
